valuation_methodology.owner_cash_definition: FY2025 company-defined free cash flow less share-based compensation; fixed-asset purchases and related payable changes are deducted
valuation_methodology.decision_rule: Use operating returns, a complete component schedule, and reverse expectations as separate views. Every material component receives a low/base/high estimate; do not average the views.

component_valuation.methodology_note: Every material component receives a low/base/high present-value estimate. Range width reflects evidence quality, realization timing, and commercial uncertainty; it is not a zero-value placeholder.

components.label: Producing royalties, easements, and land-resource operations

valuation.evidence: FY2025 Land & Resource net income plus D&A, with 3%/2% growth and 20x terminal value; 12%/10%/8% discount-rate range.
valuation.cross_check: FY2025 royalties $411.7M and easements $78.2M; producing economics are fully captured here.

valuation.evidence: May 2026 investor presentation: FY2025 Water post-tax cash flow $177M less $20M/$15M/$10M maintenance capex = $2.15/$2.35/$2.55 per share. Low/base/high use 2%/4%/6% early growth, 10x/12x/14x terminal values, and 12%/10%/8% discount rates.
valuation.cross_check: FY2025 water revenue $307.5M, water-sales revenue $170M, produced-water royalty revenue $124M with essentially zero direct operating expense or capex, and net PP&E $164.5M. Growth capex remains excluded from maintenance owner cash and must earn an explicit return.

valuation.evidence: FY2025 net PP&E of $164.5M; value is a floor cross-check and is embedded in Water operations, not additive.
valuation.cross_check: Operating DCF of Water Services already capitalizes returns on this infrastructure.
No debt is disclosed in the FY2025 balance-sheet bridge.

valuation.evidence: 224,000 NRA multiplied by the March 2025 177-NRA acquisition mark of $19,775/NRA, then risked at 15%/25%/35% for development timing, quality, taxes, and realization.
valuation.cross_check: The range is deliberately below the un-risked $64.2/sh transaction extrapolation.

valuation.evidence: 882,000 acres at $750/$1,500/$2,500 per acre. The May 2025 787-acre sale at $5,718/acre is a ceiling cross-check, not a portfolio-wide mark.
valuation.cross_check: The base is materially below the observed small-parcel sale and requires a future acreage-use segmentation refresh.

valuation.evidence: Reserve for taxes, transaction costs, and corporate friction when latent land and royalty value is realized; no debt is separately deducted.
valuation.cross_check: Low case absorbs more of the gross option value as realization cost.

inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 company-defined FCF $498.333M less $13.817M share-based compensation = $484.516M / 69.027M diluted shares (10-K FY2025)
inputs.book_per_share_caveat: GAAP book excludes Assigned 1888 land and royalty interests (zero on balance sheet); not a fair-value or dhando floor
inputs.normalization_note: Owner cash deducts $59.531M fixed-asset purchases, $6.417M increase in related payables, and $13.817M share-based compensation. Acquisitions and the $50M equity-method investment are excluded from recurring owner cash.

optionality_gate.floor_value_note: Complete component schedule includes risked low/base/high estimates for undeveloped NRA and surface acreage; it is not a liquidation guarantee.
optionality_gate.overlay_nav_note: Legacy $210.5 estimate retired because it mixed operating value with thinly supported acreage extrapolations
optionality_gate.notes: Operating return remains the stance gate. The component schedule separately estimates every material asset and reserve.

nav_overlay.retirement_note: Legacy illustrative marks retained for audit history only. They do not enter valuation_views or the stance gate.

gaap_vs_fair_value.policy_source: 10-K FY2025: fair market value not determined in 1888; no value assigned to Assigned land and royalty interests
gaap_vs_fair_value.stockholders_equity_note: Mostly accumulated operating earnings; not land mark-to-market

not_in_model_requires_refresh: Water infrastructure replacement cost vs PPE net $164.5M — book used in SOTP; RC uplift [HUMAN REVIEW]
Full NRA roll-up separating producing vs undeveloped acres — marginal comp only

mar_2025_nra_acquisition.caveat: Marginal proved/acquired NRA only; assigned 1888 interests still at zero on BS

may_2025_land_sale.caveat: Single transaction; not representative of full 882k surface portfolio

segments_or_options.label: Undeveloped / incremental NRA royalty option
segments_or_options.evidence: 224k NRA × $19,775 Mar 2025 comp; P=25% for incremental drilling not in FY2025 run-rate

sotp_build.double_count_note: Producing royalties/easements/water are in segment PV; NRA comp and surface are incremental asset marks — do not add segment PV + full NRA comp without adjustment
sotp_build.tie_out: Price $393 vs overlay base ~$211/sh → market embeds Permian scarcity premium and growth beyond filing-backed marks; Lawrence base -1.0% on current OCF remains stance gate.

segment_build.allocation_method: FY2025 segment net income plus segment D&A, less fixed-asset purchases allocated to Water; $6.555M tax-timing reconciliation held separately

segments.label: Land & Resource (royalties + easements + land sales)
segments.owner_cash_y0_source: FY2025 LRM net income $322.415M + D&A $44.555M = $366.970M / 69.027M shares; royalty acquisitions excluded
segments.notes: FY2025 segment $490.7M: royalties $411.7M, easements $78.2M; cyclical Permian toll

segments.label: Water Services and Operations (TPWR)
segments.owner_cash_y0_source: FY2025 WSO net income $158.961M + D&A $17.978M - fixed-asset purchases/payable change $65.948M = $110.991M / 69.027M shares
segments.notes: FY2025 Water $307.5M (+16% YoY); embedded_in_segment for water ramp — not separate zero terminal

options.label: Undeveloped NRA / 1888 Assigned acreage option
options.base_terminal_rationale: Producing royalties in Land segment; incremental undeveloped drilling valued at P=25% × NRA comp — not in Lawrence 5% growth
options.evidence: 10-K: Assigned land/RRA at zero on BS; Mar 2025 177 NRA @ $3.5M (~$19,775/NRA)

options.label: Surface acreage (882k acres)
options.base_terminal_rationale: Surface rights separate from royalty cash flow; $2,500/ac [Assumption] vs May 2025 sale $5,718/ac

reconciliation.slack_notes: Segment cross-check excludes the small consolidated tax-timing reconciliation; no asset-option value is included.

bear.notes: Permian activity weakens; water growth slows; year-7 exit multiple compresses

base.notes: Low-to-mid-single-digit owner-cash growth; water and easements offset slower royalties; year-7 exit

bull.notes: Permian volumes and water infrastructure outperform; premium year-7 toll multiple sustained

growth_explanation.theory_label: Permian producing toll + water infra ramp (undeveloped NRA excluded)
growth_explanation.problem_situation: Lawrence base IRR -1.0% at $393 on $7.91/sh OCF. Why 5% years 1-5 when FY2025 OCF grew ~11% and Q1 rev +21%?

mechanisms.chain: Permian production on TPL acreage × royalty rates → $411.7M FY2025 royalties (+10% YoY) → operating cash grows low-to-mid single digits if activity holds
TPWR water sourcing/treatment volumes on scarce acreage → $307.5M FY2025 water revenue (+16% YoY) → lifts consolidated cash above royalty-only path
Long-dated easements with CPI escalators → $78.2M FY2025 (+24% YoY) adds stable mid-single-digit cash contribution

risky_predictions.text: Water segment revenue exceeds $330M in FY2026 if 5% consolidated cash path holds
Q2–Q4 2026 consolidated revenue growth stays above 10% YoY
risky_predictions.falsifier_action: Revise growth down; activity cycle weaker than base

falsifiers.observation: Permian rig count or completion intensity on TPL counties down >15% YoY for four consecutive quarters
falsifiers.action: Cut years 1–5 growth to 2%; move toward bear case

falsifiers.observation: Water revenue growth below 8% for two consecutive quarters while royalties flat
falsifiers.action: Remove water uplift from theory; cut consolidated to 3%

falsifiers.observation: FY2026 operating cash flow below $550M (~$7.95/sh) with no one-off explanation
falsifiers.action: Falsifies 5% path; revisit base at 2% or bear

ad_hoc_rescue_banned: Raising growth to match Q1 +21% revenue burst without Permian activity bridge
Using GAAP book $21/sh as downside floor
Smuggling undeveloped-acreage NAV into consolidated FCF growth

theory_implied.derivation: segment_build owner-cash weighted blend (growth only; exit from scenarios.base)

option_scan.question: In-business option: Undeveloped NRA / 1888 Assigned acreage option?
option_scan.evidence: 10-K: Assigned land/RRA at zero on BS; Mar 2025 177 NRA @ $3.5M (~$19,775/NRA)

synthesis.reason: Correlated operating paths and unsupported asset options must not be averaged into a consensus return.

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

themes.label: AI compute -> power demand -> grid/water constraint -> scarce Permian surface

indicators.label: TPL water segment revenue (USD m, FY)

peer_context.note: Context only; percentile rank not in base IRR.

insider_signal.disclaimer: Context only. Insider activity informs scenario confidence and stance discussion; it does not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

scenario_confidence.note: Scenario weights are qualitative confidence only; Lawrence base IRR unchanged.

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory persona context only. It does not replace the operating stance gate or enter valuation_views.

valuation_views.decision_rule: Do not average operating returns, component value, and reverse expectations.

components.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

legacy_output_policy.results_lawrence_legacy: compatibility alias of the current operating scenarios; not a separate valuation

legacy_output_policy.option_scan: inventory disclosure only; asset value enters only through valuation_views.assets

component_review_queue.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?

Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?
